title: [v4.0-rhel] Bump github.com/containerd/containerd to v1.5.18
body:
Bump containerd to v1.5.18 to address CVE-2023-25173 and the Jira card:

https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-13490

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.0-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@vrothberg if you could PTAL, I'd appreciate it.  In this branch, containerd was only in go.sum.  After the vendor bump with `go get`, it's now in go.mod.  I think that's OK, but would like a confirm.

I'm also leary about doing this as this branch is not the newest, but we're supposed to make a best effort given the CVE.

What is the point of this? We do not use containerd to create containers so we cannot vulnerable by this, the changed code for this fix is not even vendored here so we 100% do not use it and I would just close it as non applicable to us.

We do had the same issue but they were already fixed as https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4wjj-jwc9-2x96 and https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-fjm8-m7m6-2fjp
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

@Luap99 and @mheon, based on the above discussions and the fact that we've made other changes to the go.mod in this branch, I decided to wipe the original commit and redo it. This seems a lot more reasonable now. Only 26 files are touched rather than almost 200, and the go.mod looks a lot better. IDK if I messed up the original vendor, but I wouldn't bet against it.

I spent a little time looking to see if we actually called any of the containerd code that was problematic, but after 30 minutes, I couldn't confirm or deny, so I took the chicken's way out and did this over.

We do not vendor any of the effected code, and we do not use any container creation code from containerd so we cannot be vulnerable by this containerd vulnerability as mentioned above. The linked issue even link our podman/buildah CVE that we fixed long ago already.

And if you look at the fix in containerd (https://github.com/containerd/containerd/commit/a62c38bf2173faa813018939710fc8491e4f7dba) there you can see the fix is in the oci package which is not even vendored by us as mentioned above.
```
$ ls vendor/github.com/containerd/containerd/oci
ls: cannot access 'vendor/github.com/containerd/containerd/oci': No such file or directory
```

@Luap99 thanks for looking into that.  I just double checked using your method and my standard grep, and I'm not seeing it either.  We also have to worry about `github.com/containerd/containerd/cri/server` but that also is not present.  I'll close this out and will backout the remaining cards.
